content: Comparing intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning against general curious agents can reveal that the intrinsic reward regularization used in ICM/RND can be repurposed to solve the active learning 'forgetting of rare surprising patterns' problem that plagues 2024-2025 SOTA memory-augmented agents. Curiosity intensity, measured as prediction error from ICM/RND, can act as a dynamic retention priority score for agent memory, automatically keeping unexpected high-error patterns that active learning often misses due to static sampling rules.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
